intro: Haplophyllum tuberculatum, also known as the desert spurge or Persian spurge, is a perennial plant species native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Haplophyllum tuberculatum include flavonoids, terpenes, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Haplophyllum tuberculatum is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions.
